steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Understand congruent triangle correspondence
steps.content: When two triangles are congruent with a stated correspondence, corresponding angles are equal. The problem specifies that E corresponds to J, so angle E equals angle J.

steps.title: Find angle J using the correspondence
steps.content: Since angle E = 45° and E corresponds to J, we have angle J = 45°. That confirms B is correct.
